en: In those days, when there was no king in Israel, a certain Levite was sojourning in the remote parts of the hill country of Ephraim, who took to himself a concubine from Bethlehem in Judah.
zh: 在以色列中沒有王的日子，有一個利未人寄居在以法蓮山地的偏遠地區；他從猶大的伯利恆給自己娶了一個女子作妾。

en: And his concubine was unfaithful to him, and she went away from him to her father's house at Bethlehem in Judah, and was there some four months.
zh: 他的妾背夫行淫，離開丈夫，回到猶大的伯利恆她父親的家那裡去了，在那裡住了四個月的日子。

en: Then her husband arose and went after her, to speak kindly to her and bring her back. He had with him his servant and a couple of donkeys. And she brought him into her father's house. And when the girl's father saw him, he came with joy to meet him.
zh: 她的丈夫起來，帶著一個僕人和兩頭驢去追她，用話打動她的心，叫她回來。她把丈夫帶到自己父家，那少女的父親見了那人，就歡歡喜喜迎接他。

en: And his father-in-law, the girl's father, made him stay, and he remained with him three days. So they ate and drank and spent the night there.
zh: 那人的岳父，就是女子的父親，強留那人，那人就與他同住了三天；他們一起吃喝，在那裡住宿。

en: And on the fourth day they arose early in the morning, and he prepared to go, but the girl's father said to his son-in-law, "Strengthen your heart with a morsel of bread, and after that you may go."
zh: 到了第四天，他們清早起來，那人起身要走；那女子的父親對女婿說：“請吃點餅增添心力，然後再走。”

en: So the two of them sat and ate and drank together. And the girl's father said to the man, "Be pleased to spend the night, and let your heart be merry."
zh: 於是二人坐下，一起吃喝；女子的父親對那人說：“請你答應再過一夜，暢快你的心。”

en: And when the man rose up to go, his father-in-law pressed him, till he spent the night there again.
zh: 那人起來要走，他的岳父強留他，他又在那裡過了一夜。

en: And on the fifth day he arose early in the morning to depart. And the girl's father said, "Strengthen your heart and wait until the day declines." So they ate, both of them.
zh: 到了第五天，他清早起來要走；那女子的父親說：“請吃點餅增添心力，等到日頭西斜再走吧。”於是他們耽延，直到日頭西斜；二人一同吃飯。

en: And when the man and his concubine and his servant rose up to depart, his father-in-law, the girl's father, said to him, "Behold, now the day has waned toward evening. Please, spend the night. Behold, the day draws to its close. Lodge here and let your heart be merry, and tomorrow you shall arise early in the morning for your journey, and go home."
zh: 那人和他的妾，以及他的僕人起來要走的時候，他的岳父，就是那女子的父親，對他說：“看哪，天快晚了！請再住一夜。看哪，日頭西斜了！請在這裡再住一夜，暢快你的心；明天可以清早起來，上路回家去。”

en: But the man would not spend the night. He rose up and departed and arrived opposite Jebus (that is, Jerusalem). He had with him a couple of saddled donkeys, and his concubine was with him.
zh: 那人不願再住一夜，就帶著自己的妾和兩頭備好了的驢，起來走了，來到耶布斯對面；耶布斯就是耶路撒冷。

en: When they were near Jebus, the day was nearly over, and the servant said to his master, "Come now, let us turn aside to this city of the Jebusites and spend the night in it."
zh: 他們走近耶布斯的時候，日頭快要落了；僕人對主人說：“來，讓我們轉到這耶布斯人的城去，好在那裡住宿。”

en: And his master said to him, "We will not turn aside into the city of foreigners, who do not belong to the people of Israel, but we will pass on to Gibeah."
zh: 主人對他說：“我們不可轉到這座不是以色列人居住的外族人的城裡去，我們過到基比亞去吧。”

en: And he said to his young man, "Come and let us draw near to one of these places and spend the night at Gibeah or at Ramah."
zh: 又對他的僕人說：“來吧，我們可以到這些地方其中一個去投宿，或在基比亞或在拉瑪。”

en: So they passed on and went their way. And the sun went down on them near Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin,
zh: 於是他們走路過去；他們走近便雅憫人的基比亞的時候，日頭已經落了。

en: and they turned aside there, to go in and spend the night at Gibeah. And he went in and sat down in the open square of the city, for no one took them into his house to spend the night.
zh: 他們從那裡轉身，要進基比亞去住宿；他們進去，坐在城裡的廣場上，因為沒有人接待他們到家裡去住宿。

en: And behold, an old man was coming from his work in the field at evening. The man was from the hill country of Ephraim, and he was sojourning in Gibeah. The men of the place were Benjaminites.
zh: 到晚上，有一個老年人從田間工作回來；那人原是以法蓮山地的人，寄居在基比亞；那地方的人卻是便雅憫人。

en: And he lifted up his eyes and saw the traveler in the open square of the city. And the old man said, "Where are you going? and where do you come from?"
zh: 那老年人舉目，看見那個旅客在城裡的廣場上，就問他：“你要到哪裡去？你從哪裡來？”

en: And he said to him, "We are passing from Bethlehem in Judah to the remote parts of the hill country of Ephraim, from which I come. I went to Bethlehem in Judah, and I am going to the house of the Lord, but no one has taken me into his house.
zh: 利未人回答他：“我們是從猶大的伯利恆過來的，要到以法蓮山地的偏遠地區去，我原是那地方的人；我去過猶大的伯利恆，現在我要到我的家去 ，但沒有人接待我到家裡去。

en: We have straw and feed for our donkeys, with bread and wine for me and your female servant and the young man with your servants. There is no lack of anything."
zh: 其實我有糧草、有飼料，可以餵驢；我和我的妾，以及與你的僕人在一起的那個青年人，都有餅和酒吃喝，一無所缺。”

en: And the old man said, "Peace be to you; I will care for all your wants. Only, do not spend the night in the square."
zh: 那老年人說：“願你平安，你所缺乏的，由我負責好了；只是不可在廣場上過夜。”

en: So he brought him into his house and gave the donkeys feed. And they washed their feet, and ate and drank.
zh: 於是把他領到自己的家裡，用飼料餵了驢，他們洗了腳，就吃喝起來。

en: As they were making their hearts merry, behold, the men of the city, worthless fellows, surrounded the house, beating on the door. And they said to the old man, the master of the house, "Bring out the man who came into your house, that we may know him."
zh: 他們心裡正暢快的時候，忽然城裡有些無賴之徒，圍繞房子，不住地敲門，對老房主說：“把進入你家的那個人帶出來，我們要與他交合。”

en: And the man, the master of the house, went out to them and said to them, "No, my brothers, do not act so wickedly; since this man has come into my house, do not do this vile thing.
zh: 那房主出來見他們，對他們說：“我的眾兄弟啊，不可這樣，請你們不要作惡；這人既然進了我的家，你們就不要作這羞恥的事。

en: Behold, here are my virgin daughter and his concubine. Let me bring them out now. Violate them and do with them what seems good to you, but against this man do not do this outrageous thing."
zh: 這裡有我的女兒，還是處女，並且有這人的妾，我把她們領出來，讓你們污辱她們；你們看怎樣好，就怎樣待她們吧。只是對這個人，你們卻不要作這羞恥的事。”

en: But the men would not listen to him. So the man seized his concubine and made her go out to them. And they knew her and abused her all night until the morning. And as the dawn began to break, they let her go.
zh: 那些人卻不肯聽從他的話，那人就抓住自己的妾，拉出外邊去交給他們，他們就與她交合，整夜污辱她；直到早晨，天色破曉的時候，才放她走。

en: And as morning appeared, the woman came and fell down at the door of the man's house where her master was, until it was light.
zh: 天快亮的時候，那女子回到留她主人住宿的那人的房子門口，就仆倒在那裡，直到天亮。

en: And her master rose up in the morning, and when he opened the doors of the house and went out to go on his way, behold, there was his concubine lying at the door of the house, with her hands on the threshold.
zh: 到早晨，她的主人起來，開了房門，出去要上路的時候，就看見那婦人，就是他的妾，仆倒在門口，她的雙手搭在門檻上。

en: He said to her, "Get up, let us be going." But there was no answer. Then he put her on the donkey, and the man rose up and went away to his home.
zh: 他對那婦人說：“起來，我們走吧！”可是卻沒有回答。那人就把她馱在驢上，起程回自己的地方去了。

en: And when he entered his house, he took a knife, and taking hold of his concubine he divided her, limb by limb, into twelve pieces, and sent her throughout all the territory of Israel.
zh: 他到了家裡，就拿起刀來，抓住自己的妾，把她的肢體切成十二塊，叫人送到以色列的全境去。

en: And all who saw it said, "Such a thing has never happened or been seen from the day that the people of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt until this day; consider it, take counsel, and speak."
zh: 看見的人都說：“自從以色列人從埃及地上來的日子，直到今日，這樣的事並沒有發生過，也沒有看見過；你們要思想，要商議，要討論。”
